description: Duplicate event detection. The same payload (same event_id, email_id, campaign_id, email_account, lead_email -> same idempotency_key) is POSTed twice. The first execution proceeds normally; the second execution must be detected as a duplicate by Workflow 1 Section E3 (idempotency.is_duplicate=true), which makes Workflow 2 Section A set opFlags.is_duplicate_event=true, noop_match=true, and route to the NOOP plan with no repeated downstream action.

input.reply_subject: Re: Quick question about your sales pipeline
input.reply_text: Yes, I'm interested - happy to chat further.
input.reply_text_snippet: Yes, I'm interested - happy to chat further.

expected.how_to_test: POST this exact input to the dev webhook twice in sequence (same payload both times). The idempotency_key is event_id:email_id:campaign_id:email_account:lead_email, all of which are identical across both submissions, so both submissions hash to the same key regardless of intake_id.

second_submission.reason_contains: Duplicate event detected for idempotency key "evt-phase3-010:email-phase3-010:campaign-validation-001:outbound1@hmzvalidation.test:robin.shaw@prospectco.test"

expected.notes: PROVISIONAL: idempotency.is_duplicate is computed in Workflow 1 Section E3 by comparing the Data Table row's createdAt vs updatedAt after an upsert keyed on idempotency_key (n8n Data Table id xUlD0Zhoek6mU5I2, table hmz_validation_reply_intake_idempotency). Whether n8n's Data Table 'upsert' operation returns row-level createdAt/updatedAt timestamps that differ between insert and update was not independently confirmed before this build (duplicate_detection_status='PROVISIONAL', logged in reports/UNRESOLVED_ITEMS.md U1). On the second submission, E4. Duplicate Event Router (Phase 3.1A Repair 4) reads idempotency.is_duplicate=true and routes directly to E5. Duplicate Event Terminal instead of F. Deterministic Prefilter - Workflow 2 (Sections A-E) never runs for the second submission, so prefilter/deterministic/classifier/decision/draft/validation/validation_learning are all absent from the result (see must_be_absent above), and E5 alone sets terminal_status=COMPLETED_NO_SEND, external_action_status=NOOP, stop_active_sequence=false, and a reason referencing the duplicated idempotency_key. If createdAt/updatedAt are not returned by the real Data Table as expected, is_duplicate may always read false and the second_submission expectations above would not be met against the live n8n instance - this remains PROVISIONAL pending live confirmation.
